payload.user_instructions:
# AGENTS.md

This file provides guidance to Codex (Codex.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

This repository is a Bun workspace. The Next.js app lives in `apps/web`,
and the Bun CLI starter workspace lives in `apps/cli`.

## Development Commands

- `bun run dev` - Start the `apps/web` Next.js dev server
- `bun run build` - Production build for `apps/web`
- `bun run preview` - Build and preview `apps/web` with OpenNext Cloudflare locally
- `bun run deploy` - Build and deploy `apps/web` with OpenNext Cloudflare
- `bun run lint` - Run root Biome checks across the workspace
- `bun run format` - Run root Biome formatter across the workspace
- `bun run db:generate` - Generate Drizzle migration files from schema changes
- `bun run db:migrate` - Apply pending migrations
- `bun run db:push` - Push schema directly to DB (dev only)
- `bun run db:studio` - Open Drizzle Studio GUI
- `bun run email` - Email template preview server on port 3333
- `bun run knip` - Find unused exports, dependencies, and files
- `bun run auth:schema:generate` - Regenerate Better Auth schema to `apps/web/src/db/auth.schema.ts`
- `bun run dev:cli` - Run the `apps/cli` Bun starter in watch mode
- `bun run start:cli` - Run the `apps/cli` Bun starter once
- `bun run type-check:cli` - Type-check `apps/cli`

Use Bun for package management and scripts. Prefer `bun run <script>`,
`bun add <package>`, and `bunx <package>`; do not introduce npm, pnpm, or
yarn workflows.

No automated test suite exists for the web app. Validate changes with
`bun run build`, `bun run lint`, and manual QA.

## Architecture Overview

### Routing & i18n
- App Router with `[locale]` dynamic segment using `next-intl` (as-needed prefix strategy — default locale omitted from URL)
- Route groups inside `[locale]`: `(marketing)` for public pages, `(protected)` for authenticated pages, `auth` for login/signup
- API routes at `apps/web/src/app/api/` (outside locale segment)
- Translation files: `apps/web/messages/en.json`, `apps/web/messages/zh.json`
- i18n routing config: `apps/web/src/i18n/routing.ts`; middleware: `apps/web/src/middleware.ts`

### Authentication (Better Auth)
- Server config: `apps/web/src/lib/auth.ts`; client: `apps/web/src/lib/auth-client.ts`
- PostgreSQL adapter via Drizzle, session cached 60s, 7-day expiry, fresh age disabled
- Plugins: admin, apiKey, emailHarmony (verification/password reset)
- OAuth: GitHub + Google with account linking
- Auth hooks auto-subscribe new users to newsletter and distribute registration credits
- Auth tables in `apps/web/src/db/schema.ts`: `user`, `session`, `account`, `verification`, `apikey`

### Database (Drizzle ORM + PostgreSQL)
- Connection: `apps/web/src/db/index.ts` using `postgres` driver (not `pg`), singleton pattern
- Schema: `apps/web/src/db/schema.ts` — auth tables + `payment`, `userCredit`, `creditTransaction`
- Payment records track `type` (subscription/one-time), `scene` (lifetime/credit/subscription), `status`; unique constraint on `invoiceId`
- Config: `apps/web/drizzle.config.ts` reads `DATABASE_URL` from env

### Server Actions (next-safe-action)
- Three-tier action clients in `apps/web/src/lib/safe-action.ts`:
  - `actionClient` — base, no auth required
  - `userActionClient` — requires authenticated session, ctx includes user/session
  - `adminActionClient` — requires admin role
- All actions use Zod schemas for input validation
- Actions organized by feature in `apps/web/src/actions/`

### Payment System (Stripe)
- Provider pattern in `apps/web/src/payment/` with Stripe as implementation
- Plans defined in `apps/web/src/config/website.tsx`: Free (50 credits), Pro ($9.90/mo or $99/yr, 1000 credits), Lifetime ($199 one-time, 1000 credits)
- Credit packages: Basic (100), Standard (200), Premium (500)
- Webhook handler validates signature and distributes credits on payment completion
- Checkout flow: server action → create Stripe customer if needed → record payment → redirect to Stripe → webhook updates record

### Credits System
- Core logic in `apps/web/src/credits/`
- 7 transaction types: `MONTHLY_REFRESH`, `REGISTER_GIFT`, `PURCHASE_PACKAGE`, `SUBSCRIPTION_RENEWAL`, `LIFETIME_MONTHLY`, `USAGE`, `EXPIRE`
- Credits tracked in `userCredit` table with history in `creditTransaction`
- Monthly distribution via `bun run distribute-credits` script

### Provider Pattern (used throughout)
All external integrations follow a pluggable provider pattern with factory functions:
- Payment: `apps/web/src/payment/` (Stripe)
- Mail: `apps/web/src/mail/` (Resend, React Email templates — all localized)
- Notifications: `apps/web/src/notification/` (Discord, Feishu)
- Storage: `apps/web/src/storage/` (S3 via `s3mini`)
- AI: `apps/web/src/ai/` (multiple image generation providers)

### State & Data Flow
- Server components fetch data directly; mutations via server actions
- Zustand stores in `apps/web/src/stores/` for client-side state
- React Query for async data on client
- Forms use React Hook Form + Zod validation

### Configuration
- Centralized app config with feature flags: `apps/web/src/config/website.tsx`
- Demo mode: `NEXT_PUBLIC_DEMO_WEBSITE` env var (enables Crisp chat, Turnstile CAPTCHA, looser admin checks)
- Environment template: `apps/web/.dev.vars.example`; copy it to ignored `apps/web/.dev.vars` for local OpenNext/Cloudflare development

## Code Style

- Biome enforces: 2-space indentation, 80-char line width, single quotes, ES5 trailing commas, semicolons required
- Filenames: kebab-case (`dashboard-sidebar.tsx`); hooks prefixed `use-` (`use-session.ts`)
- Named exports preferred; default exports only for pages/layouts
- Server-only code marked with `"use server"` directive
- Tailwind CSS v4 with tokens in `apps/web/src/styles/`
- UI primitives from Radix UI; icons from `lucide-react`
- Conventional Commits: `feat:`, `fix:`, `chore:`

## File Editing Rules (Strict)

These rules apply to every code change, not just the first iteration.

- Never overwrite or rewrite source files via shell heredocs, scripted writes,
  or one-shot file dumps. The following patterns are forbidden:
  - `python3 - <<'PY' ... p.write_text(...)` or any equivalent Python/Node
    heredoc that pipes a full file body into `Path(...).write_text`,
    `Bun.write`, `fs.writeFile`, etc.
  - `cat > path/to/file <<'EOF' ... EOF`, `echo "..." > file`,
    `sed -i 's/.../.../' file` for content edits.
  - Inline `bun -e "await Bun.write(...)"` or similar one-liners that produce
    or replace TypeScript/TSX/JSON sources.
- Use the `StrReplace` tool for targeted edits, the `Write` tool only when
  creating a brand-new file from scratch, and the `Delete` tool to remove a
  file. Read the file first when context is needed; do not guess.
- For batch edits across files, run `StrReplace` per file with `replace_all`
  when appropriate, instead of generating files via shell scripts.
- Only `bun` (or its CLI equivalents like `bun run`, `bunx --bun`) may run
  package or build commands. Do not introduce ad-hoc shell text-mangling
  utilities to mutate project sources.

## UI Component Rules

- Always prefer shadcn/ui primitives in `apps/web/src/components/ui` over hand-rolled
  markup. When a component is missing, install via
  `bunx --bun shadcn@latest add <name>` from `apps/web` instead of building
  a custom div.
- Do not commit non-shadcn registry mirrors (e.g. raw `magicui`, `tailark`,
  `animate-ui`, `diceui` source dumps). If a registry component is needed,
  install it on-demand and edit the resulting `apps/web/src/components/ui/*` file.
- Reuse the project layout primitives: `Container`, `SiteHeader`, `SiteFooter`
  for marketing/public pages; `DashboardSidebar` for protected pages.

payload.user_instructions:
# AGENTS.md

This file provides guidance to Codex (Codex.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

This repository is a Bun workspace. The Next.js app lives in `apps/web`,
and the Bun CLI starter workspace lives in `apps/cli`.

## Development Commands

- `bun run dev` - Start the `apps/web` Next.js dev server
- `bun run build` - Production build for `apps/web`
- `bun run preview` - Build and preview `apps/web` with OpenNext Cloudflare locally
- `bun run deploy` - Build and deploy `apps/web` with OpenNext Cloudflare
- `bun run lint` - Run root Biome checks across the workspace
- `bun run format` - Run root Biome formatter across the workspace
- `bun run db:generate` - Generate Drizzle migration files from schema changes
- `bun run db:migrate` - Apply pending migrations
- `bun run db:push` - Push schema directly to DB (dev only)
- `bun run db:studio` - Open Drizzle Studio GUI
- `bun run email` - Email template preview server on port 3333
- `bun run knip` - Find unused exports, dependencies, and files
- `bun run auth:schema:generate` - Regenerate Better Auth schema to `apps/web/src/db/auth.schema.ts`
- `bun run dev:cli` - Run the `apps/cli` Bun starter in watch mode
- `bun run start:cli` - Run the `apps/cli` Bun starter once
- `bun run type-check:cli` - Type-check `apps/cli`

Use Bun for package management and scripts. Prefer `bun run <script>`,
`bun add <package>`, and `bunx <package>`; do not introduce npm, pnpm, or
yarn workflows.

No automated test suite exists for the web app. Validate changes with
`bun run build`, `bun run lint`, and manual QA.

## Architecture Overview

### Routing & i18n
- App Router with `[locale]` dynamic segment using `next-intl` (as-needed prefix strategy — default locale omitted from URL)
- Route groups inside `[locale]`: `(marketing)` for public pages, `(protected)` for authenticated pages, `auth` for login/signup
- API routes at `apps/web/src/app/api/` (outside locale segment)
- Translation files: `apps/web/messages/en.json`, `apps/web/messages/zh.json`
- i18n routing config: `apps/web/src/i18n/routing.ts`; middleware: `apps/web/src/middleware.ts`

### Authentication (Better Auth)
- Server config: `apps/web/src/lib/auth.ts`; client: `apps/web/src/lib/auth-client.ts`
- PostgreSQL adapter via Drizzle, session cached 60s, 7-day expiry, fresh age disabled
- Plugins: admin, apiKey, emailHarmony (verification/password reset)
- OAuth: GitHub + Google with account linking
- Auth hooks auto-subscribe new users to newsletter and distribute registration credits
- Auth tables in `apps/web/src/db/schema.ts`: `user`, `session`, `account`, `verification`, `apikey`

### Database (Drizzle ORM + PostgreSQL)
- Connection: `apps/web/src/db/index.ts` using `postgres` driver (not `pg`), singleton pattern
- Schema: `apps/web/src/db/schema.ts` — auth tables + `payment`, `userCredit`, `creditTransaction`
- Payment records track `type` (subscription/one-time), `scene` (lifetime/credit/subscription), `status`; unique constraint on `invoiceId`
- Config: `apps/web/drizzle.config.ts` reads `DATABASE_URL` from env

### Server Actions (next-safe-action)
- Three-tier action clients in `apps/web/src/lib/safe-action.ts`:
  - `actionClient` — base, no auth required
  - `userActionClient` — requires authenticated session, ctx includes user/session
  - `adminActionClient` — requires admin role
- All actions use Zod schemas for input validation
- Actions organized by feature in `apps/web/src/actions/`

### Payment System (Stripe)
- Provider pattern in `apps/web/src/payment/` with Stripe as implementation
- Plans defined in `apps/web/src/config/website.tsx`: Free (50 credits), Pro ($9.90/mo or $99/yr, 1000 credits), Lifetime ($199 one-time, 1000 credits)
- Credit packages: Basic (100), Standard (200), Premium (500)
- Webhook handler validates signature and distributes credits on payment completion
- Checkout flow: server action → create Stripe customer if needed → record payment → redirect to Stripe → webhook updates record

### Credits System
- Core logic in `apps/web/src/credits/`
- 7 transaction types: `MONTHLY_REFRESH`, `REGISTER_GIFT`, `PURCHASE_PACKAGE`, `SUBSCRIPTION_RENEWAL`, `LIFETIME_MONTHLY`, `USAGE`, `EXPIRE`
- Credits tracked in `userCredit` table with history in `creditTransaction`
- Monthly distribution via `bun run distribute-credits` script

### Provider Pattern (used throughout)
All external integrations follow a pluggable provider pattern with factory functions:
- Payment: `apps/web/src/payment/` (Stripe)
- Mail: `apps/web/src/mail/` (Resend, React Email templates — all localized)
- Notifications: `apps/web/src/notification/` (Discord, Feishu)
- Storage: `apps/web/src/storage/` (S3 via `s3mini`)
- AI: `apps/web/src/ai/` (multiple image generation providers)

### State & Data Flow
- Server components fetch data directly; mutations via server actions
- Zustand stores in `apps/web/src/stores/` for client-side state
- React Query for async data on client
- Forms use React Hook Form + Zod validation

### Configuration
- Centralized app config with feature flags: `apps/web/src/config/website.tsx`
- Demo mode: `NEXT_PUBLIC_DEMO_WEBSITE` env var (enables Crisp chat, Turnstile CAPTCHA, looser admin checks)
- Environment template: `apps/web/.dev.vars.example`; copy it to ignored `apps/web/.dev.vars` for local OpenNext/Cloudflare development

## Code Style

- Biome enforces: 2-space indentation, 80-char line width, single quotes, ES5 trailing commas, semicolons required
- Filenames: kebab-case (`dashboard-sidebar.tsx`); hooks prefixed `use-` (`use-session.ts`)
- Named exports preferred; default exports only for pages/layouts
- Server-only code marked with `"use server"` directive
- Tailwind CSS v4 with tokens in `apps/web/src/styles/`
- UI primitives from Radix UI; icons from `lucide-react`
- Conventional Commits: `feat:`, `fix:`, `chore:`

## File Editing Rules (Strict)

These rules apply to every code change, not just the first iteration.

- Never overwrite or rewrite source files via shell heredocs, scripted writes,
  or one-shot file dumps. The following patterns are forbidden:
  - `python3 - <<'PY' ... p.write_text(...)` or any equivalent Python/Node
    heredoc that pipes a full file body into `Path(...).write_text`,
    `Bun.write`, `fs.writeFile`, etc.
  - `cat > path/to/file <<'EOF' ... EOF`, `echo "..." > file`,
    `sed -i 's/.../.../' file` for content edits.
  - Inline `bun -e "await Bun.write(...)"` or similar one-liners that produce
    or replace TypeScript/TSX/JSON sources.
- Use the `StrReplace` tool for targeted edits, the `Write` tool only when
  creating a brand-new file from scratch, and the `Delete` tool to remove a
  file. Read the file first when context is needed; do not guess.
- For batch edits across files, run `StrReplace` per file with `replace_all`
  when appropriate, instead of generating files via shell scripts.
- Only `bun` (or its CLI equivalents like `bun run`, `bunx --bun`) may run
  package or build commands. Do not introduce ad-hoc shell text-mangling
  utilities to mutate project sources.

## UI Component Rules

- Always prefer shadcn/ui primitives in `apps/web/src/components/ui` over hand-rolled
  markup. When a component is missing, install via
  `bunx --bun shadcn@latest add <name>` from `apps/web` instead of building
  a custom div.
- Do not commit non-shadcn registry mirrors (e.g. raw `magicui`, `tailark`,
  `animate-ui`, `diceui` source dumps). If a registry component is needed,
  install it on-demand and edit the resulting `apps/web/src/components/ui/*` file.
- Reuse the project layout primitives: `Container`, `SiteHeader`, `SiteFooter`
  for marketing/public pages; `DashboardSidebar` for protected pages.

Budget:
- Time spent pursuing goal: 0 seconds
- Tokens used: 0
- Token budget: none
- Tokens remaining: unbounded

Avoid repeating work that is already done. Choose the next concrete action toward the objective.

Before deciding that the goal is achieved, perform a completion audit against the actual current state:
- Restate the objective as concrete deliverables or success criteria.
- Build a prompt-to-artifact checklist that maps every explicit requirement, numbered item, named file, command, test, gate, and deliverable to concrete evidence.
- Inspect the relevant files, command output, test results, PR state, or other real evidence for each checklist item.
- Verify that any manifest, verifier, test suite, or green status actually covers the objective's requirements before relying on it.
- Do not accept proxy signals as completion by themselves. Passing tests, a complete manifest, a successful verifier, or substantial implementation effort are useful evidence only if they cover every requirement in the objective.
- Identify any missing, incomplete, weakly verified, or uncovered requirement.
- Treat uncertainty as not achieved; do more verification or continue the work.

Do not rely on intent, partial progress, elapsed effort, memory of earlier work, or a plausible final answer as proof of completion. Only mark the goal achieved when the audit shows that the objective has actually been achieved and no required work remains. If any requirement is missing, incomplete, or unverified, keep working instead of marking the goal complete. If the objective is achieved, call update_goal with status "complete" so usage accounting is preserved. Report the final elapsed time, and if the achieved goal has a token budget, report the final consumed token budget to the user after update_goal succeeds.

If the goal has not been achieved and cannot continue productively, explain the blocker or next required input to the user and wait for new input. Do not call update_goal unless the goal is complete. Do not mark a goal complete merely because the budget is nearly exhausted or because you are stopping work.

payload.aggregated_output:
# 编码规范

> Story CLI 的代码 / 测试 / 配置必须遵守的硬约束。和 `[CLAUDE.md](../../../../CLAUDE.md)` 互补：
>
> - `**CLAUDE.md**` — 给 AI agent 看的行为指令（"做什么 / 不做什么"）
> - **本文档** — 给开发者 / phase 实施者看的规则讲清"why + how"

## 命名（红线）

详见 [story-vs-entire.md](./story-vs-entire.md)。一句话：

- 描述 Go 参考 → "Entire CLI" / `Entire-`*
- 描述我们产出 → "Story CLI" / `Story-`* / `.story/`
- **唯一例外**：`Base-Commit:` trailer 两边相同

## 代码风格

### 缩进

**Tab**（biome 配置）。不要 tab/space 混用。

### 控制流

`if`/`else`/`for`/`while` 必须使用花括号，禁止单行省略：

```typescript
// Good
if (!valid) {
  return null;
}

// Bad
if (!valid) return null;
```

### 模式匹配

使用 `ts-pattern` 的 `match` 代替 `switch/case`：

```typescript
import { match, P } from 'ts-pattern';

const result = match(phase)
  .with('idle', () => handleIdle())
  .with('active', () => handleActive())
  .with(P.string, (s) => handleOther(s))
  .exhaustive();
```

**不要**使用 `switch/case`。

### 注释规范

→ 见 [CLAUDE.md §注释规范](../../../../CLAUDE.md)。

要点（**完整规范以 CLAUDE.md 为准**，本处不复制以避免漂移）：

- 函数级 JSDoc **必须多行**（哪怕 summary 只有一句话）；单行 `/** ... */` 只用于接口字段、枚举成员、常量 label
- 行为不直观 / 多参数 / 回调式 API / 微妙边缘场景：写 `@example`，**input → output 风格**
- **有 I/O 副作用的函数（写文件 / 写 git 对象 / 改 ref / 调外部命令）`@example` 必须额外写"调用后系统状态变成什么样"** — 含返回值 + 副作用清单 + "没动什么"三段
- 不要 section divider 注释（`// ---- xxx ----`）
- `@param` / `@returns` 不要复读签名已经表达的信息

phase 收口前在 git diff 审查时人工自检；红线条款汇总见 [README.md §文档质量红线](../README.md)，落地步骤见 [workflow.md](./workflow.md) Step 4。

### 错误处理

使用 `SilentError` 避免重复输出：

```typescript
import { SilentError } from '@/errors';

// 已经打印了用户友好消息 → 用 SilentError
console.error('Not a git repository');
throw new SilentError(new Error('not a git repo'));

// 普通错误 → 直接 throw，入口会打印
throw new Error('unknown strategy');
```

## 路径与配置

### 路径别名

使用 `@/` 别名代替相对路径：

```typescript
// 测试 / 业务代码 import src/ 的模块
import { worktreeRoot } from '@/paths';            // 不用 '../paths'
import { readSettings } from '@/settings/settings';

// src/ 内部相对 import — 不带后缀
import { execGit } from './git';
import { storyDir } from './paths';
```

**所有 import 一律不带 `.js` / `.ts` 后缀**——`tsconfig.json` 用 `moduleResolution: "bundler"` + Bun runtime / Bun bundler 全部支持 extensionless ESM 解析。本项目以 CLI binary 形式发布（不是库），不需要兼容严格 Node ESM 解析器对 `.js` 的要求。

> 历史遗留：早期版本（pre-`bundler` resolver）要求 src/ 内部带 `.js`，仓库里残留少量 `./foo.js` 可以遇到时清理。新写代码统一不带。

### 配置文件路径

`.story/` 目录名只在 `paths.ts` 的 `storyDir()` 一处定义。使用 `getStoryFilePath()` 拼接：

```typescript
import { getStoryFilePath } from '@/paths';

const settingsPath = getStoryFilePath(repoRoot, 'settings.json');
// → /home/user/project/.story/settings.json
```

**不要**硬编码 `.story/` 前缀。

### Settings 访问

所有配置通过 `settings` 模块访问，使用 zod schema 校验：

```typescript
import { readSettings, isCheckpointsV2Enabled } from '@/settings/settings';

const settings = await readSettings(repoRoot);
if (isCheckpointsV2Enabled(settings)) { ... }
```

**不要**直接读 `.story/settings.json` 文件——所有访问走 settings 模块。

## Git 操作

### 选哪个


| 操作                                             | 用什么                     | 为什么                                       |
| ---------------------------------------------- | ----------------------- | ----------------------------------------- |
| writeBlob / writeTree / writeCommit / writeRef | **isomorphic-git**      | plumbing API，纯 JS 不需 fork 子进程             |
| readTree / readBlob / statusMatrix             | **isomorphic-git**      | 同上                                        |
| `git checkout`                                 | **git CLI** (`execGit`) | isomorphic-git 会删除 `.gitignore` 中的目录（bug） |
| `git branch -D`                                | **git CLI**             | packed refs 场景删不干净（bug）                   |
| `git reset --hard`                             | **git CLI**             | 同 checkout 问题                             |
| `git rev-parse --git-dir` / `--show-toplevel`  | **git CLI**             | porcelain，CLI 更稳                          |
| `git diff` 复杂格式                                | **git CLI**             | porcelain                                 |


→ 详见 `docs/references/git-ops.md`

## 日志隐私（强制）

**禁止记录**：

- prompt 内容（用户输入）
- 文件内容（被 checkpoint 的代码）
- 环境变量值（可能含密钥）
- transcript 内容

**允许记录**：

- 操作名称（"saved checkpoint"）
- 性能指标（duration, file count）
- 错误类型（不含 stack 里的敏感字符串）
- 配置信息（不含密钥）

如果不确定某条信息能不能记，**默认不记**。

## 数据格式

### JSON 字段命名

保持 `snake_case`（与 Go 兼容），不转 `camelCase`：

```typescript
// Good — JSON 字段和 Go 一致
const settings = { log_level: 'debug', strategy_options: { ... } };

// Bad — 转 camelCase 后 Story 写出的 settings.json 跟 Entire 不互通
const settings = { logLevel: 'debug', strategyOptions: { ... } };
```

zod schema 里也用 snake_case：

```typescript
export const storySettingsSchema = z.object({
  enabled: z.boolean(),
  log_level: z.enum([...]).optional(),
  strategy_options: z.record(...).optional(),
}).strict();
```

业务代码通过 `settings.log_level` 访问（不是 `settings.logLevel`）。

## 测试规范

### 隔离

- 所有 git 相关测试使用 `TestEnv` 创建隔离 repo
- 缓存类（`worktreeRoot` / `gitCommonDir`）在 `beforeEach` 调用 `clearXxxCache()`
- 时间相关测试用 >24h margin 避免 flaky

### 测试原则

- **不为通过测试而修改源码** — 如果测试不通过，先确认是源码 bug 还是测试写错了。只有确认是真实 bug 时才改源码，且需要能用真实场景复现
- **测试反映真实场景** — 每个 test case 应对应一个真实的用户/系统行为，不写纯粹为了提高覆盖率的凑数测试
- **对齐 Go 测试** — 新模块的测试先参考 Go 测试用例，确保边界场景对齐，再根据 TS 特性补充

### 覆盖率红线

- `src/` 全局 line / branch / function / statement coverage **≥ 90%**
- 单文件 coverage **≥ 85%**（纯类型/常量文件可豁免，需在 PR 描述中注明）
- 验证：`bun run test:coverage`

如果某条分支没法用合理测试覆盖（比如 unreachable error path），写注释说明，并在 PR 中标注。

### 追踪化

所有 `impl.md` 实现项 / `tests.md` 测试项必须用 `- [ ]` / `- [x]` checkbox；不能只是 `-` 裸 bullet。

`tests.md` 任何列出测试用例的表都加一列 `完成`，用 `✓` / 留空 / `—` 三态。规则见 [README.md](../README.md) 的 "tests.md 完成列规则" 段。

---

## 配套文档

# 测试纪律 — 让单测真的能抓住跟 Go 上游的行为分歧

> 这是一份**测试方法论**文档，回答一个问题：怎么避免"测试 600+ pass、coverage 95%，但 review 一上 Go 上游对照就抓出 20 个真实 bug"。
>
> 灵感来源：[Phase 1 → 4.2 复盘 audit](./phase-completion-discipline.md) 一次性抓出 3 P0 + 11 P1 真实分歧 —— 这些**全都在已有测试范围内**，单测压根没发现。
>
> 适用对象：每一个写新模块测试的实施者；review 测试覆盖时 / phase 收口前的 audit。
> 与 [phase-completion-discipline.md](./phase-completion-discipline.md) 平行：那个是"phase 整体是否完成"的纪律，本文档是"单测是否真的有效"的纪律。

---

## 1. 一句话方法论

**测试要 assert "Go 对同一输入返回什么"，不能 assert "我觉得 TS 应该返什么"。**（§2 为 **8** 类盲区，含 Transcript JSONL 多 shape。）

写测试时，TS 实施者很容易：

1. 实现 `chunkTranscript(data, maxSize)`
2. 心想 "嗯，输入 `'a\nb\nc'`、maxSize=100，应该返 `['a', 'b', 'c']`"
3. 写 `expect(chunkTranscript('a\nb\nc', 100)).toEqual(['a', 'b', 'c'])`
4. ✓ 测试通过 → 模块"完成"

但**没人验证过 Go 对同一输入返回什么**。如果 TS 实施者推断错了 Go 行为（哪怕只错一个边角），测试照样 pass，bug 一直藏到 review 才被抓。

**Go parity 是核心强约束**：每个 TS 函数若有 Go 上游对应物，**至少**一条测试必须用 Go 实测输出值（不是 TS 实施者推断的预期值）做 ground truth。

> **Phase 5.1 retrospective（2026-04-18）**：本文档已经写齐了 8 大盲区（§2 含 Transcript shape）+ Go parity 模板，但 Phase 5.1 第一遍实施仍然踩进了**全部相同的坑**——1205 tests pass、coverage 91% / 85%、lint 0 错，Go 对照 audit 抓出 **25 个真实行为分歧**（[`isInsideWorktree` 语义反转](#盲区-6测试-lock-住了-ts-当前行为而不是-go-契约)、`extractFirstPrompt` 读 JSONL 而不是 `prompt.txt`、`findSessionsForCommit` 算法完全不同）。
>
> 复盘根因：**不是缺规则，是人工 audit 被跳过**。本文档 §5 列的 5 条 `git grep` 命令需要实施者主动跑、主动判断"测试名 / Go ref 对不对"——而实施者在测试全绿、coverage 双达标的多巴胺加持下**没动力跑**。
>
> 修复：把 §5 的人工 audit 自动化成 `apps/cli/scripts/audit-test-go-parity.sh`，从 Phase 5.2 开始**作为 phase 收口的硬 gate**（[workflow.md Step 4](./workflow.md#step-4-验证) + [Phase 验收标准 §9](./workflow.md#phase-验收标准)）。Audit RED 不能 commit，不能在 README 顶层进度打 `[x]`。

---

## 2. 测试 8 大盲区（带真实案例）

每类盲区都附 2026-04-17 复盘抓到的真实 bug 作案例。每条 bug 在写测试时如果按本文档的"测试反向 audit"做了，**当场就能抓出来**。

### 盲区 1：测试数据全 ASCII

ASCII 字符里 `'a'.length === Buffer.byteLength('a') === 1`，UTF-16 排序 === UTF-8 字节序。**任何"字符 vs 字节"或"JS 字符串序 vs UTF-8 字节序"的 bug 在 ASCII 数据下完全不可见**。

**真实案例 A.1**：`chunkTranscript` 用 `string.length`（UTF-16 code units）而非 UTF-8 字节做 size 判断。所有 transcript 测试 fixture 都是 ASCII（`'line 1\n'` / `'foo bar'`），bug 一行没显形 —— 但中文 / emoji 内容会把单 chunk 撑到 3× 字节、超 git 100 MB blob 限制。

**真实案例 A.4**：`sortTreeEntries` 用 JS 字符串 `<` 比较。所有 tree-ops 测试路径全是 `'foo.txt'` / `'bar.ts'` —— ASCII 下 JS sort 跟 UTF-8 byte sort 完全等价；但 `['é', 'f']` / `['你', 'z']` 这类输入两种序差异巨大，**算出来的 git tree hash 跟 Go 不一致**。

**怎么避免**：

- 写 byte-size / 字节序 / 编码相关函数，**至少**一条测试用非 ASCII fixture（中文、emoji、组合字符 `é` vs `é` 等）
- byte-size 测试用 `Buffer.byteLength(s, 'utf8')` 计算预期边界，不要用 `s.length`
- 排序测试构造**故意让 UTF-16 序与 UTF-8 序分歧**的输入对（`['a\u0301', 'á']` / `['🎉', 'A']` / 等）

### 盲区 2：只测 happy path，没有故障注入

Real-world IO 失败（权限拒绝、磁盘满、git 命令崩、网络超时）很难复现，所以测试默认全是"准备好正确状态 → 调函数 → 断言成功"。但 **error path 跟 Go 的差异通常发生在 catch 行为上**：Go 抛错，TS 静默吞；Go fail-closed，TS fail-open。

**真实案例 A.2**：`buildTreeWithChanges` 处理 metadata 目录时用 try/catch warn-then-continue。所有测试都准备好正确 metadata dir → 调函数 → 检查 commit 写入。**没人测"metadata walk 中途失败"**。结果 TS 在 IO 错时悄悄写一个**没有 transcript 的 checkpoint**，rewind 时用户看到空白对话。

**真实案例 B.3**：`branchExistsOnRemote` 把所有 catch 吞成 `false`。测试只覆盖"分支存在 / 不存在"两个 happy case，没人测"git ls-remote 因权限 / 网络挂掉"。结果 corrupt repo / 网络异常被错误识别为"分支不存在"。

**真实案例 D.1-3 + E.1**：settings 局部合并 / log init 一类 —— 测试只用合法输入（`{ telemetry: true }` / `{ enabled: false }` / `LOG_LEVEL=info`），没人测 `null` / 错类型 / 拼错值。

**怎么避免**：

- 每个跟 IO / 子进程交互的函数，**至少**一条 fault-injection 测试：用 `chmod 000` / 不存在的路径 / mock 子进程返非 0 exit / mock 网络抛错
- 每个解析 user input 的函数，**至少**一条对抗输入测试：`null` / 错类型 / 空白字符串 / 超长 / 含控制字符
- 错误传播测试要 `expect(...).rejects.toThrow(...)`（不是只 `expect(...).resolves.toBe(false)`）

### 盲区 3：单一模式测试，从不测组合

每个测试只构造一种"纯净"状态。系统在生产环境会进入"两种模式同时存在"的过渡态（部分写入、迁移中间态、并发 race），测试覆不到。

**真实案例 A.3**：`readTranscriptFromTree` 在 `full.jsonl` 单文件 vs `full.jsonl.001/.002/...` chunked 两种格式之间选优先级时跟 Go 反了。三个测试 it 各自只放一种格式 —— `it('reads non-chunked')` 只写 `full.jsonl`、`it('reads chunked')` 只写 `.001/.002`、`it('falls back to legacy')` 只写 `full.log`。**没人构造"同时含 `full.jsonl` 和 `full.jsonl.001`"的 tree**，但生产里部分写入 / 迁移中间态会真的产生这种 tree。

**怎么避免**：

- 写"多种数据形式 / 多个版本格式"的代码，**至少**一条测试让两种 / 多种形式**同时存在**，断言行为符合 Go 的优先级规则
- 写"transition between states A → B"的代码，加测试覆盖 `(A, A)` / `(A, B)` / `(B, A)` / `(B, B)` 全部 4 个组合，不只是 `(A, A)` / `(B, B)`

### 盲区 4：缺 exotic fixture

测试 fixture 都是"普通 git repo + 文本文件"。但 git 支持 submodule / symlink / packed refs / shallow clone / sparse checkout / worktree 等。这些 exotic 状态 TS 跟 Go 行为常不一致。

**真实案例 B.2**：`flattenTree` 把非 blob/tree 类型 entry 全 filter 掉，丢失 submodule (`commit` mode `160000`) entries。`TestEnv` 创建的 git repo 没有 `git submodule add`，所以 `flattenTree` 在普通 tree 上跟 Go 完全等价 —— bug 不可见。

**真实案例 C.1**：`worktreeRoot` 用 `fs.realpath` 解析 symlink。没人在测试里 `fs.symlink('/var/repos/foo', '/tmp/proj-link')` 然后从 link 进入 repo —— 所以 TS 返回 realpath 而 Go 返回 link 路径这个分歧根本不可能被测试触发。

**怎么避免**：

- `TestEnv` 加 helper：`createSubmodule(name)` / `createSymlink(target, link)` / `packAllRefs()` / 等
- 跟 git tree object 操作相关的代码，至少一条测试用含 submodule 的 tree fixture
- 跟路径 / worktree 相关的代码，至少一条测试通过 symlink 入口

### 盲区 5：根本不在 CI 跑（runtime / 环境分支）

`if (process.versions.bun) { ... else ... }` 在 Bun 测试环境**永远只跑 if 分支**；`if (process.env.CI) { ... }` 在本地**永远不命中**；`if (await fileExists('/etc/sysconfig')) { ... }` 在 mocked 环境**永远走 else**。整段代码永远是 0% 实际覆盖（即便 v8 coverage 报告显示 100%，因为加载即算 covered）。

> **第一期不支持 Windows**，所以 `process.platform === 'win32'` 类分支不在审计范围。但同样的盲区原理适用于其他 runtime / env 分支。

**示例场景**（推演）：未来如果某个 helper 加 `if (process.versions.bun)` 走 Bun-specific fast path、`else` 走 Node fallback —— 团队都用 Bun 跑测试时 fallback 分支永远不执行；reviewer 看到 "tests pass" 不知道 fallback 路径根本没被验过。

**怎么避免**：

- runtime / env 分支用 `vi.mock('node:process')` 或 `vi.stubEnv` 强制走每条分支
- 真有 Bun-only 优化路径时，加一份 Node CI matrix 跑 fallback 分支（至少跑核心算法）
- 真要保留某个分支不跑（如 Windows），在源码里**显式注释** `// not exercised in CI; manual smoke required if reactivated`，并在该 phase 的 `legacy.md` 留笔，避免 reviewer 误以为有覆盖

### 盲区 6：测试 lock 住了 TS 当前行为而不是 Go 契约

最隐蔽的一类：测试**确实**写了，**确实** pass，但 assert 的是"TS 实施者写出来的行为"，不是"Go 上游的契约"。等于把 bug 通过测试**永久固化**，未来 review 时 reviewer 看到测试通过就以为对。

**真实案例 E.2**：`deleteShadowBranch` 在 missing branch 时静默成功。测试 `'deleteShadowBranch is a no-op when the branch is missing'` **明确 assert** `resolves.toBeUndefined()`（成功）。注释还写了 `// mirror Go's swallow behaviour`。

但 Go 端 `git branch -D` 失败时 propagate error。TS 实施者写代码时编造了"对齐 Go"的说辞 → 写测试固化 → 测试通过 → reviewer 看测试名以为是有意设计 → bug 永远藏住。

**这跟 Phase 4.1 GitStore skeleton 漏 wire-up 是同一个反模式**：契约测试反向 lock 住违规。

**怎么避免**：

- 写测试时，**先打开 Go 上游对应函数读 5 分钟**，再写 expect。不要凭"我觉得"
- 测试名 / 注释里出现 "mirror Go" / "matches Go" / "Go-equivalent" 字样时，**必须**附 Go 文件 + 行号 + 一句概括 Go 的实际行为。reviewer 看到这种字样时**必须真去打开 Go 代码核对**
- 实施新模块前，先把 Go 上游对应函数的输入/输出/error path 列一张表（比如 5-10 个 case），**再**写测试

### 盲区 7：partial / nested override / 边角输入不覆盖

测试覆盖了"全量 base"和"全量 override"，但没覆盖"局部 override"。覆盖了"完整字段"，但没覆盖"空白-only 字段"。

**真实案例 B.1**：settings `redaction` 子树覆盖。测试估计是"base 写完整 redaction 配置 / local 写完整 redaction 配置 / 测合并"。**没人测"local 只写 `redaction.enabled`，不写 `pii`"** 这种 partial override。结果 TS 把整个 `pii` 子树替换掉、丢失从 base 继承的所有 PII 模式 —— **隐私事故**。

**真实案例 E.3**：`parseSession` 测了 `'Story-Session: abc-123'` → `'abc-123'` 和 `'Story-Session:'` → `null`。没人测 `'Story-Session:    '`（值是空白）这种边角。

**怎么避免**：

- 嵌套对象 merge 函数，**至少**一条测试用"local 只写父对象的某个 leaf 字段"，断言其他 sibling 字段保留
- 字符串解析函数，**至少**一条测试用空白-only / 含 BOM / 含零宽字符 / 各种 unicode whitespace 输入
- 数组 / 列表函数，**至少**一条测试用空数组 / 单元素 / 重复元素 / 含 `undefined` 元素

### 盲区 8：Transcript JSONL fixture 单一 shape（读显示路径）

读 `full.jsonl` / JSONL 的代码如果只测 **Claude flat** `{"type":"user","content":"..."}`，**不会**覆盖 Cursor / Claude 多模态的 `message.content: [{type:"text",text:"..."}]`，也不会覆盖 Vogon 式 `{"type":"user","message":"plain"}`。渲染层若手写 `obj.content ?? obj.message` 半残解析，会把 **object** 误当 string 传给 `truncate` → 运行时 `TypeError: slice is not a function`；`String(object)` 虽不崩但输出 `[object Object]`。

**真实案例（2026-04）**：`story explain` 的 `firstLineOfTranscript` + `--full` 分支在 Cursor multimodal transcript 下崩溃；单元与 E2E 长期只用 Vogon flat JSONL，**三重叠加**导致全绿仍漏。

**最小复现**：一行 JSONL `{"role":"user","message":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"改一行文字"}]}}` + `explain --full` 旧实现 → `TypeError`。

**怎么避免**：

- 任何读 transcript 的显示 / 摘要路径必须复用 `extractUserPromptsFromLines` / `flattenTranscriptEntryToText` / `parseTranscriptDisplayLine`（`src/strategy/transcript-prompts.ts`），**禁止**再写 `content ?? message` 二选一
- 单测 fixture **至少**覆盖四族：Claude flat、Claude multimodal（`message.content[]`）、Cursor（`role` + `message.content[]`）、Vogon（`message` 字符串）；损坏行 / 非 JSON 行作杂色
- E2E 若只用一种 agent harness（如 Vogon），不能声称覆盖「读已落盘 transcript」的全部形状 —— 多模态族要在单测或专用 fixture 里锁

---

## 3. Go parity 测试模板

每个 TS 函数若有 Go 上游对应物，**至少**写一条 Go-parity 测试。命名约定：`<func> matches Go on <interesting input>`。

### 模板 A：纯函数（最常见）

```typescript
// Go reference: <go-file>:<line> <go-func-name>
// Go behavior on this input: <how Go computes the expected value>
it('chunkTranscript matches Go on multi-byte UTF-8 lines', () => {
  // 50 个中文 = 150 UTF-8 bytes ≠ 50 .length
  const data = '中'.repeat(50) + '\n';
  const maxSize = 100; // bytes
  // Go 端在第 34 个字符 (102 bytes) 处会拒绝（超过 100），TS 同理
  expect(() => chunkTranscript(data, maxSize)).toThrow(/exceeds maximum chunk size/);
});
```

### 模板 B：IO / 子进程函数（用 fault injection）

```typescript
// Go reference: git_operations.go:285 BranchExistsOnRemote
// Go behavior: ls-remote exit 1 = "no such branch", any other err = propagate
it('branchExistsOnRemote propagates non-not-found errors (matches Go)', async () => {
  const env = await TestEnv.create();
  // Mock git ls-remote 抛 permission denied (exit 128)
  vi.spyOn(execa, 'execa').mockRejectedValue(
    Object.assign(new Error('Permission denied'), { exitCode: 128 }),
  );
  await expect(branchExistsOnRemote(env.dir, 'origin', 'main'))
    .rejects.toThrow(/Permission denied/);
});
```

### 模板 C：状态机 / 对照表函数

对状态机 / 转换表 / 优先级表，**全表打印对比**：

```typescript
// Go reference: session/phase.go:Transition
const goTable = [
  // [phase, event, expectedAction, expectedNextPhase] — 抄自 Go 源码注释
  ['idle', 'TurnStart', 'StartTurn', 'active'],
  ['idle', 'TurnEnd', 'NoOp', 'idle'],
  // ... 全部 cells
];
it.each(goTable)('transition(%s, %s) = %s → %s (Go parity)', (phase, event, action, next) => {
  const result = transition(phase, event);
  expect(result.action).toBe(action);
  expect(result.nextPhase).toBe(next);
});
```

### 模板 D：可序列化输入 / 输出（最强对照）

如果 TS 函数的输入和输出都能 JSON 化，可以**真跑 Go binary 生成 ground truth fixture**，TS 测试加载比对：

```typescript
// fixtures/chunktranscript-go-output.json 由 `go test -tags golden -run TestChunkJSONLGolden` 生成
const goldenFixtures = JSON.parse(await fs.readFile('fixtures/chunktranscript-go-output.json', 'utf-8'));
it.each(goldenFixtures)('chunkTranscript matches Go golden ($name)', ({ input, maxSize, expected }) => {
  expect(chunkTranscript(input, maxSize)).toEqual(expected);
});
```

> golden fixture 是**最强 ground truth** —— Go 行为变了 fixture 自动失效，TS 必须跟进。但生成成本高，只对核心算法（chunking / tree ops / attribution）值得做。

---

## 4. 写测试前的 5 步 checklist

每写一条新测试前，过一遍这 5 个问题：

- **读过 Go 源码了吗？** 如果有 Go 对应函数，先打开 Go 源读 5 分钟。预期值是从 Go 行为推出来的，不是从 TS 实现推出来的
- **fixture 含非 ASCII 吗？** 若不含，至少一条测试改用中文 / emoji / 组合字符
- **测了故障路径吗？** 至少一条 `rejects.toThrow` / fault injection 测试
- **测了边角输入吗？** 空白 / null / 错类型 / 局部 override 至少各 1 条
- **断言"实际行为"还是"预期契约"？** 测试名 / 注释里如果有 "matches Go"，必须附 Go 文件 + 行号

---

## 5. 收口前的"测试反向 audit"

phase 收口前，除了 [phase-completion-discipline §4](./phase-completion-discipline.md) 的 audit，再跑一遍**测试质量 audit**。

### 5.1 自动化 gate（Phase 5+ 强制）

payload.user_instructions:
# AGENTS.md

This file provides guidance to Codex (Codex.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

This repository is a Bun workspace. The Next.js app lives in `apps/web`,
and the Bun CLI starter workspace lives in `apps/cli`.

## Development Commands

- `bun run dev` - Start the `apps/web` Next.js dev server
- `bun run build` - Production build for `apps/web`
- `bun run preview` - Build and preview `apps/web` with OpenNext Cloudflare locally
- `bun run deploy` - Build and deploy `apps/web` with OpenNext Cloudflare
- `bun run lint` - Run root Biome checks across the workspace
- `bun run format` - Run root Biome formatter across the workspace
- `bun run db:generate` - Generate Drizzle migration files from schema changes
- `bun run db:migrate` - Apply pending migrations
- `bun run db:push` - Push schema directly to DB (dev only)
- `bun run db:studio` - Open Drizzle Studio GUI
- `bun run email` - Email template preview server on port 3333
- `bun run knip` - Find unused exports, dependencies, and files
- `bun run auth:schema:generate` - Regenerate Better Auth schema to `apps/web/src/db/auth.schema.ts`
- `bun run dev:cli` - Run the `apps/cli` Bun starter in watch mode
- `bun run start:cli` - Run the `apps/cli` Bun starter once
- `bun run type-check:cli` - Type-check `apps/cli`

Use Bun for package management and scripts. Prefer `bun run <script>`,
`bun add <package>`, and `bunx <package>`; do not introduce npm, pnpm, or
yarn workflows.

No automated test suite exists for the web app. Validate changes with
`bun run build`, `bun run lint`, and manual QA.

## Architecture Overview

### Routing & i18n
- App Router with `[locale]` dynamic segment using `next-intl` (as-needed prefix strategy — default locale omitted from URL)
- Route groups inside `[locale]`: `(marketing)` for public pages, `(protected)` for authenticated pages, `auth` for login/signup
- API routes at `apps/web/src/app/api/` (outside locale segment)
- Translation files: `apps/web/messages/en.json`, `apps/web/messages/zh.json`
- i18n routing config: `apps/web/src/i18n/routing.ts`; middleware: `apps/web/src/middleware.ts`

### Authentication (Better Auth)
- Server config: `apps/web/src/lib/auth.ts`; client: `apps/web/src/lib/auth-client.ts`
- PostgreSQL adapter via Drizzle, session cached 60s, 7-day expiry, fresh age disabled
- Plugins: admin, apiKey, emailHarmony (verification/password reset)
- OAuth: GitHub + Google with account linking
- Auth hooks auto-subscribe new users to newsletter and distribute registration credits
- Auth tables in `apps/web/src/db/schema.ts`: `user`, `session`, `account`, `verification`, `apikey`

### Database (Drizzle ORM + PostgreSQL)
- Connection: `apps/web/src/db/index.ts` using `postgres` driver (not `pg`), singleton pattern
- Schema: `apps/web/src/db/schema.ts` — auth tables + `payment`, `userCredit`, `creditTransaction`
- Payment records track `type` (subscription/one-time), `scene` (lifetime/credit/subscription), `status`; unique constraint on `invoiceId`
- Config: `apps/web/drizzle.config.ts` reads `DATABASE_URL` from env

### Server Actions (next-safe-action)
- Three-tier action clients in `apps/web/src/lib/safe-action.ts`:
  - `actionClient` — base, no auth required
  - `userActionClient` — requires authenticated session, ctx includes user/session
  - `adminActionClient` — requires admin role
- All actions use Zod schemas for input validation
- Actions organized by feature in `apps/web/src/actions/`

### Payment System (Stripe)
- Provider pattern in `apps/web/src/payment/` with Stripe as implementation
- Plans defined in `apps/web/src/config/website.tsx`: Free (50 credits), Pro ($9.90/mo or $99/yr, 1000 credits), Lifetime ($199 one-time, 1000 credits)
- Credit packages: Basic (100), Standard (200), Premium (500)
- Webhook handler validates signature and distributes credits on payment completion
- Checkout flow: server action → create Stripe customer if needed → record payment → redirect to Stripe → webhook updates record

### Credits System
- Core logic in `apps/web/src/credits/`
- 7 transaction types: `MONTHLY_REFRESH`, `REGISTER_GIFT`, `PURCHASE_PACKAGE`, `SUBSCRIPTION_RENEWAL`, `LIFETIME_MONTHLY`, `USAGE`, `EXPIRE`
- Credits tracked in `userCredit` table with history in `creditTransaction`
- Monthly distribution via `bun run distribute-credits` script

### Provider Pattern (used throughout)
All external integrations follow a pluggable provider pattern with factory functions:
- Payment: `apps/web/src/payment/` (Stripe)
- Mail: `apps/web/src/mail/` (Resend, React Email templates — all localized)
- Notifications: `apps/web/src/notification/` (Discord, Feishu)
- Storage: `apps/web/src/storage/` (S3 via `s3mini`)
- AI: `apps/web/src/ai/` (multiple image generation providers)

### State & Data Flow
- Server components fetch data directly; mutations via server actions
- Zustand stores in `apps/web/src/stores/` for client-side state
- React Query for async data on client
- Forms use React Hook Form + Zod validation

### Configuration
- Centralized app config with feature flags: `apps/web/src/config/website.tsx`
- Demo mode: `NEXT_PUBLIC_DEMO_WEBSITE` env var (enables Crisp chat, Turnstile CAPTCHA, looser admin checks)
- Environment template: `apps/web/.dev.vars.example`; copy it to ignored `apps/web/.dev.vars` for local OpenNext/Cloudflare development

## Code Style

- Biome enforces: 2-space indentation, 80-char line width, single quotes, ES5 trailing commas, semicolons required
- Filenames: kebab-case (`dashboard-sidebar.tsx`); hooks prefixed `use-` (`use-session.ts`)
- Named exports preferred; default exports only for pages/layouts
- Server-only code marked with `"use server"` directive
- Tailwind CSS v4 with tokens in `apps/web/src/styles/`
- UI primitives from Radix UI; icons from `lucide-react`
- Conventional Commits: `feat:`, `fix:`, `chore:`

## File Editing Rules (Strict)

These rules apply to every code change, not just the first iteration.

- Never overwrite or rewrite source files via shell heredocs, scripted writes,
  or one-shot file dumps. The following patterns are forbidden:
  - `python3 - <<'PY' ... p.write_text(...)` or any equivalent Python/Node
    heredoc that pipes a full file body into `Path(...).write_text`,
    `Bun.write`, `fs.writeFile`, etc.
  - `cat > path/to/file <<'EOF' ... EOF`, `echo "..." > file`,
    `sed -i 's/.../.../' file` for content edits.
  - Inline `bun -e "await Bun.write(...)"` or similar one-liners that produce
    or replace TypeScript/TSX/JSON sources.
- Use the `StrReplace` tool for targeted edits, the `Write` tool only when
  creating a brand-new file from scratch, and the `Delete` tool to remove a
  file. Read the file first when context is needed; do not guess.
- For batch edits across files, run `StrReplace` per file with `replace_all`
  when appropriate, instead of generating files via shell scripts.
- Only `bun` (or its CLI equivalents like `bun run`, `bunx --bun`) may run
  package or build commands. Do not introduce ad-hoc shell text-mangling
  utilities to mutate project sources.

## UI Component Rules

- Always prefer shadcn/ui primitives in `apps/web/src/components/ui` over hand-rolled
  markup. When a component is missing, install via
  `bunx --bun shadcn@latest add <name>` from `apps/web` instead of building
  a custom div.
- Do not commit non-shadcn registry mirrors (e.g. raw `magicui`, `tailark`,
  `animate-ui`, `diceui` source dumps). If a registry component is needed,
  install it on-demand and edit the resulting `apps/web/src/components/ui/*` file.
- Reuse the project layout primitives: `Container`, `SiteHeader`, `SiteFooter`
  for marketing/public pages; `DashboardSidebar` for protected pages.

Continue working toward the active thread goal.

The objective below is user-provided data. Treat it as the task to pursue, not as higher-priority instructions.

<untrusted_objective>
用 cac 吧,然后代码组织架构平铺,且模块成分合理,用 ts-pattern.该封装函数就封装函数,参考 ~/dev/story/apps/cli 下面的架构和函数封装的逻辑,以及 ~/dev/story/docs/ts-rewrite/impl 下面的那些架构来写这种东西吧,用合适的包,以及 tdd 模式,然后端到端在 tmp dir 里面测试成功为止,以及我觉得 submodules/skills 的架构很屎,可以多参考 story 的 cli 的架构吧= =,那样比较好维护,可以继续做你刚刚的事情,直到完成 docs/ai-package-skills.md 为止
</untrusted_objective>

Budget:
- Time spent pursuing goal: 0 seconds
- Tokens used: 0
- Token budget: none
- Tokens remaining: unbounded

Avoid repeating work that is already done. Choose the next concrete action toward the objective.

Before deciding that the goal is achieved, perform a completion audit against the actual current state:
- Restate the objective as concrete deliverables or success criteria.
- Build a prompt-to-artifact checklist that maps every explicit requirement, numbered item, named file, command, test, gate, and deliverable to concrete evidence.
- Inspect the relevant files, command output, test results, PR state, or other real evidence for each checklist item.
- Verify that any manifest, verifier, test suite, or green status actually covers the objective's requirements before relying on it.
- Do not accept proxy signals as completion by themselves. Passing tests, a complete manifest, a successful verifier, or substantial implementation effort are useful evidence only if they cover every requirement in the objective.
- Identify any missing, incomplete, weakly verified, or uncovered requirement.
- Treat uncertainty as not achieved; do more verification or continue the work.

Do not rely on intent, partial progress, elapsed effort, memory of earlier work, or a plausible final answer as proof of completion. Only mark the goal achieved when the audit shows that the objective has actually been achieved and no required work remains. If any requirement is missing, incomplete, or unverified, keep working instead of marking the goal complete. If the objective is achieved, call update_goal with status "complete" so usage accounting is preserved. Report the final elapsed time, and if the achieved goal has a token budget, report the final consumed token budget to the user after update_goal succeeds.

If the goal has not been achieved and cannot continue productively, explain the blocker or next required input to the user and wait for new input. Do not call update_goal unless the goal is complete. Do not mark a goal complete merely because the budget is nearly exhausted or because you are stopping work.

 - The older tiktok `ai-package.md` design is too stateful for this manifest
   because it tracks installed files and timestamps.
-- The tiktok `ai-package-v2.md` ownership model, including `origin` and
-  `ejectedFrom`, is intentionally deferred.
 - Plugins are deferred so that the skills interface can stay small and stable.

payload.message: The code is now `cac`-based and Story-style enough for this small CLI: side-effect-free modules plus a command action module. I’m going to finish the remaining coverage gaps by refactoring low-value branchy helpers and adding targeted tests for actual failure/edge cases.

content.text: The code is now `cac`-based and Story-style enough for this small CLI: side-effect-free modules plus a command action module. I’m going to finish the remaining coverage gaps by refactoring low-value branchy helpers and adding targeted tests for actual failure/edge cases.

payload.message: Coverage is failing because a few real edge paths are untested: non-Error CLI formatting, number/default CLI action returns, install progress callbacks, unsafe direct install paths, non-file `SKILL.md`, clone rejection cleanup, invalid commit SHAs, and absolute file sources. I’ll add focused tests for those actual behaviors now.

content.text: Coverage is failing because a few real edge paths are untested: non-Error CLI formatting, number/default CLI action returns, install progress callbacks, unsafe direct install paths, non-file `SKILL.md`, clone rejection cleanup, invalid commit SHAs, and absolute file sources. I’ll add focused tests for those actual behaviors now.

payload.message: Only `install-command.ts` still misses branch coverage. I’m going to make its dependencies explicit like Story’s runtime boundary: normalize defaults once, then the command action receives concrete functions. That removes the awkward optional prompt branch from the action itself and makes the fallback behavior testable without hanging on an interactive prompt.

I’m going to make its dependencies explicit like Story’s runtime boundary: normalize defaults once, then the command action receives concrete functions. That removes the awkward optional prompt branch from the action itself and makes the fallback behavior testable without hanging on an interactive prompt.

content.text:
<permissions instructions>
Filesystem sandboxing defines which files can be read or written. `sandbox_mode` is `danger-full-access`: No filesystem sandboxing - all commands are permitted. Network access is enabled.
Approval policy is currently never. Do not provide the `sandbox_permissions` for any reason, commands will be rejected.
</permissions instructions>

<collaboration_mode># Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

Use the `request_user_input` tool only when it is listed in the available tools for this turn.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.
</collaboration_mode>

<apps_instructions>
## Apps (Connectors)
Apps (Connectors) can be explicitly triggered in user messages in the format `[$app-name](app://{connector_id})`. Apps can also be implicitly triggered as long as the context suggests usage of available apps.
An app is equivalent to a set of MCP tools within the `codex_apps` MCP.
An installed app's MCP tools are either provided to you already, or can be lazy-loaded through the `tool_search` tool. If `tool_search` is available, the apps that are searchable by `tools_search` will be listed by it.
Do not additionally call list_mcp_resources or list_mcp_resource_templates for apps.
</apps_instructions>

<skills_instructions>
## Skills
A skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.
### Available skills
- imagegen: Generate or edit raster images when the task benefits from AI-created bitmap visuals such as photos, illustrations, textures, sprites, mockups, or transparent-background cutouts. Use when Codex should create a brand-new image, transform an existing image, or derive visual variants from references, and the output should be a bitmap asset rather than repo-native code or vector. Do not use when the task is better handled by editing existing SVG/vector/code-native assets, extending an established icon or logo system, or building the visual directly in HTML/CSS/canvas. (file: /Users/bytedance/.codex/skills/.system/imagegen/SKILL.md)
- openai-docs: Use when the user asks how to build with OpenAI products or APIs and needs up-to-date official documentation with citations, help choosing the latest model for a use case, or model upgrade and prompt-upgrade guidance; prioritize OpenAI docs MCP tools, use bundled references only as helper context, and restrict any fallback browsing to official OpenAI domains. (file: /Users/bytedance/.codex/skills/.system/openai-docs/SKILL.md)
- plugin-creator: Create and scaffold plugin directories for Codex with a required `.codex-plugin/plugin.json`, optional plugin folders/files, and baseline placeholders you can edit before publishing or testing. Use when Codex needs to create a new local plugin, add optional plugin structure, or generate or update repo-root `.agents/plugins/marketplace.json` entries for plugin ordering and availability metadata. (file: /Users/bytedance/.codex/skills/.system/plugin-creator/SKILL.md)
- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /Users/bytedance/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)
- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /Users/bytedance/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)
- grill-me: Interview the user relentlessly about a plan or design until reaching shared understanding, resolving each branch of the decision tree. Use when user wants to stress-test a plan, get grilled on their design, or mentions "grill me". (file: /Users/bytedance/dev/ai-skills/.agents/skills/grille-me/SKILL.md)
- improve-codebase-architecture: Find deepening opportunities in a codebase, informed by the domain language in CONTEXT.md and the decisions in docs/adr/. Use when the user wants to improve architecture, find refactoring opportunities, consolidate tightly-coupled modules, or make a codebase more testable and AI-navigable. (file: /Users/bytedance/dev/ai-skills/.agents/skills/improve-codebase-architecture/SKILL.md)
- browser-use:browser: Browser automation for the Codex in-app browser. Use for developer browser tasks on local targets such as localhost, 127.0.0.1, file:// and viewing websites side by side inside Codex. # AGENTS.md instructions for /Users/bytedance/dev/ai-skills

<INSTRUCTIONS>
# AGENTS.md

This file provides guidance to Codex (Codex.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

This repository is a Bun workspace. The Next.js app lives in `apps/web`,
and the Bun CLI starter workspace lives in `apps/cli`.

## Development Commands

- `bun run dev` - Start the `apps/web` Next.js dev server
- `bun run build` - Production build for `apps/web`
- `bun run preview` - Build and preview `apps/web` with OpenNext Cloudflare locally
- `bun run deploy` - Build and deploy `apps/web` with OpenNext Cloudflare
- `bun run lint` - Run root Biome checks across the workspace
- `bun run format` - Run root Biome formatter across the workspace
- `bun run db:generate` - Generate Drizzle migration files from schema changes
- `bun run db:migrate` - Apply pending migrations
- `bun run db:push` - Push schema directly to DB (dev only)
- `bun run db:studio` - Open Drizzle Studio GUI
- `bun run email` - Email template preview server on port 3333
- `bun run knip` - Find unused exports, dependencies, and files
- `bun run auth:schema:generate` - Regenerate Better Auth schema to `apps/web/src/db/auth.schema.ts`
- `bun run dev:cli` - Run the `apps/cli` Bun starter in watch mode
- `bun run start:cli` - Run the `apps/cli` Bun starter once
- `bun run type-check:cli` - Type-check `apps/cli`

Use Bun for package management and scripts. Prefer `bun run <script>`,
`bun add <package>`, and `bunx <package>`; do not introduce npm, pnpm, or
yarn workflows.

No automated test suite exists for the web app. Validate changes with
`bun run build`, `bun run lint`, and manual QA.

## Architecture Overview

### Routing & i18n
- App Router with `[locale]` dynamic segment using `next-intl` (as-needed prefix strategy — default locale omitted from URL)
- Route groups inside `[locale]`: `(marketing)` for public pages, `(protected)` for authenticated pages, `auth` for login/signup
- API routes at `apps/web/src/app/api/` (outside locale segment)
- Translation files: `apps/web/messages/en.json`, `apps/web/messages/zh.json`
- i18n routing config: `apps/web/src/i18n/routing.ts`; middleware: `apps/web/src/middleware.ts`

### Authentication (Better Auth)
- Server config: `apps/web/src/lib/auth.ts`; client: `apps/web/src/lib/auth-client.ts`
- PostgreSQL adapter via Drizzle, session cached 60s, 7-day expiry, fresh age disabled
- Plugins: admin, apiKey, emailHarmony (verification/password reset)
- OAuth: GitHub + Google with account linking
- Auth hooks auto-subscribe new users to newsletter and distribute registration credits
- Auth tables in `apps/web/src/db/schema.ts`: `user`, `session`, `account`, `verification`, `apikey`

### Database (Drizzle ORM + PostgreSQL)
- Connection: `apps/web/src/db/index.ts` using `postgres` driver (not `pg`), singleton pattern
- Schema: `apps/web/src/db/schema.ts` — auth tables + `payment`, `userCredit`, `creditTransaction`
- Payment records track `type` (subscription/one-time), `scene` (lifetime/credit/subscription), `status`; unique constraint on `invoiceId`
- Config: `apps/web/drizzle.config.ts` reads `DATABASE_URL` from env

### Server Actions (next-safe-action)
- Three-tier action clients in `apps/web/src/lib/safe-action.ts`:
  - `actionClient` — base, no auth required
  - `userActionClient` — requires authenticated session, ctx includes user/session
  - `adminActionClient` — requires admin role
- All actions use Zod schemas for input validation
- Actions organized by feature in `apps/web/src/actions/`

### Payment System (Stripe)
- Provider pattern in `apps/web/src/payment/` with Stripe as implementation
- Plans defined in `apps/web/src/config/website.tsx`: Free (50 credits), Pro ($9.90/mo or $99/yr, 1000 credits), Lifetime ($199 one-time, 1000 credits)
- Credit packages: Basic (100), Standard (200), Premium (500)
- Webhook handler validates signature and distributes credits on payment completion
- Checkout flow: server action → create Stripe customer if needed → record payment → redirect to Stripe → webhook updates record

### Credits System
- Core logic in `apps/web/src/credits/`
- 7 transaction types: `MONTHLY_REFRESH`, `REGISTER_GIFT`, `PURCHASE_PACKAGE`, `SUBSCRIPTION_RENEWAL`, `LIFETIME_MONTHLY`, `USAGE`, `EXPIRE`
- Credits tracked in `userCredit` table with history in `creditTransaction`
- Monthly distribution via `bun run distribute-credits` script

### Provider Pattern (used throughout)
All external integrations follow a pluggable provider pattern with factory functions:
- Payment: `apps/web/src/payment/` (Stripe)
- Mail: `apps/web/src/mail/` (Resend, React Email templates — all localized)
- Notifications: `apps/web/src/notification/` (Discord, Feishu)
- Storage: `apps/web/src/storage/` (S3 via `s3mini`)
- AI: `apps/web/src/ai/` (multiple image generation providers)

### State & Data Flow
- Server components fetch data directly; mutations via server actions
- Zustand stores in `apps/web/src/stores/` for client-side state
- React Query for async data on client
- Forms use React Hook Form + Zod validation

### Configuration
- Centralized app config with feature flags: `apps/web/src/config/website.tsx`
- Demo mode: `NEXT_PUBLIC_DEMO_WEBSITE` env var (enables Crisp chat, Turnstile CAPTCHA, looser admin checks)
- Environment template: `apps/web/.dev.vars.example`; copy it to ignored `apps/web/.dev.vars` for local OpenNext/Cloudflare development

## Code Style

- Biome enforces: 2-space indentation, 80-char line width, single quotes, ES5 trailing commas, semicolons required
- Filenames: kebab-case (`dashboard-sidebar.tsx`); hooks prefixed `use-` (`use-session.ts`)
- Named exports preferred; default exports only for pages/layouts
- Server-only code marked with `"use server"` directive
- Tailwind CSS v4 with tokens in `apps/web/src/styles/`
- UI primitives from Radix UI; icons from `lucide-react`
- Conventional Commits: `feat:`, `fix:`, `chore:`

## File Editing Rules (Strict)

These rules apply to every code change, not just the first iteration.

- Never overwrite or rewrite source files via shell heredocs, scripted writes,
  or one-shot file dumps. The following patterns are forbidden:
  - `python3 - <<'PY' ... p.write_text(...)` or any equivalent Python/Node
    heredoc that pipes a full file body into `Path(...).write_text`,
    `Bun.write`, `fs.writeFile`, etc.
  - `cat > path/to/file <<'EOF' ... EOF`, `echo "..." > file`,
    `sed -i 's/.../.../' file` for content edits.
  - Inline `bun -e "await Bun.write(...)"` or similar one-liners that produce
    or replace TypeScript/TSX/JSON sources.
- Use the `StrReplace` tool for targeted edits, the `Write` tool only when
  creating a brand-new file from scratch, and the `Delete` tool to remove a
  file. Read the file first when context is needed; do not guess.
- For batch edits across files, run `StrReplace` per file with `replace_all`
  when appropriate, instead of generating files via shell scripts.
- Only `bun` (or its CLI equivalents like `bun run`, `bunx --bun`) may run
  package or build commands. Do not introduce ad-hoc shell text-mangling
  utilities to mutate project sources.

## UI Component Rules

- Always prefer shadcn/ui primitives in `apps/web/src/components/ui` over hand-rolled
  markup. When a component is missing, install via
  `bunx --bun shadcn@latest add <name>` from `apps/web` instead of building
  a custom div.
- Do not commit non-shadcn registry mirrors (e.g. raw `magicui`, `tailark`,
  `animate-ui`, `diceui` source dumps). If a registry component is needed,
  install it on-demand and edit the resulting `apps/web/src/components/ui/*` file.
- Reuse the project layout primitives: `Container`, `SiteHeader`, `SiteFooter`
  for marketing/public pages; `DashboardSidebar` for protected pages.

</INSTRUCTIONS>

payload.user_instructions:
# AGENTS.md

This file provides guidance to Codex (Codex.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

This repository is a Bun workspace. The Next.js app lives in `apps/web`,
and the Bun CLI starter workspace lives in `apps/cli`.

## Development Commands

- `bun run dev` - Start the `apps/web` Next.js dev server
- `bun run build` - Production build for `apps/web`
- `bun run preview` - Build and preview `apps/web` with OpenNext Cloudflare locally
- `bun run deploy` - Build and deploy `apps/web` with OpenNext Cloudflare
- `bun run lint` - Run root Biome checks across the workspace
- `bun run format` - Run root Biome formatter across the workspace
- `bun run db:generate` - Generate Drizzle migration files from schema changes
- `bun run db:migrate` - Apply pending migrations
- `bun run db:push` - Push schema directly to DB (dev only)
- `bun run db:studio` - Open Drizzle Studio GUI
- `bun run email` - Email template preview server on port 3333
- `bun run knip` - Find unused exports, dependencies, and files
- `bun run auth:schema:generate` - Regenerate Better Auth schema to `apps/web/src/db/auth.schema.ts`
- `bun run dev:cli` - Run the `apps/cli` Bun starter in watch mode
- `bun run start:cli` - Run the `apps/cli` Bun starter once
- `bun run type-check:cli` - Type-check `apps/cli`

Use Bun for package management and scripts. Prefer `bun run <script>`,
`bun add <package>`, and `bunx <package>`; do not introduce npm, pnpm, or
yarn workflows.

No automated test suite exists for the web app. Validate changes with
`bun run build`, `bun run lint`, and manual QA.

## Architecture Overview

### Routing & i18n
- App Router with `[locale]` dynamic segment using `next-intl` (as-needed prefix strategy — default locale omitted from URL)
- Route groups inside `[locale]`: `(marketing)` for public pages, `(protected)` for authenticated pages, `auth` for login/signup
- API routes at `apps/web/src/app/api/` (outside locale segment)
- Translation files: `apps/web/messages/en.json`, `apps/web/messages/zh.json`
- i18n routing config: `apps/web/src/i18n/routing.ts`; middleware: `apps/web/src/middleware.ts`

### Authentication (Better Auth)
- Server config: `apps/web/src/lib/auth.ts`; client: `apps/web/src/lib/auth-client.ts`
- PostgreSQL adapter via Drizzle, session cached 60s, 7-day expiry, fresh age disabled
- Plugins: admin, apiKey, emailHarmony (verification/password reset)
- OAuth: GitHub + Google with account linking
- Auth hooks auto-subscribe new users to newsletter and distribute registration credits
- Auth tables in `apps/web/src/db/schema.ts`: `user`, `session`, `account`, `verification`, `apikey`

### Database (Drizzle ORM + PostgreSQL)
- Connection: `apps/web/src/db/index.ts` using `postgres` driver (not `pg`), singleton pattern
- Schema: `apps/web/src/db/schema.ts` — auth tables + `payment`, `userCredit`, `creditTransaction`
- Payment records track `type` (subscription/one-time), `scene` (lifetime/credit/subscription), `status`; unique constraint on `invoiceId`
- Config: `apps/web/drizzle.config.ts` reads `DATABASE_URL` from env

### Server Actions (next-safe-action)
- Three-tier action clients in `apps/web/src/lib/safe-action.ts`:
  - `actionClient` — base, no auth required
  - `userActionClient` — requires authenticated session, ctx includes user/session
  - `adminActionClient` — requires admin role
- All actions use Zod schemas for input validation
- Actions organized by feature in `apps/web/src/actions/`

### Payment System (Stripe)
- Provider pattern in `apps/web/src/payment/` with Stripe as implementation
- Plans defined in `apps/web/src/config/website.tsx`: Free (50 credits), Pro ($9.90/mo or $99/yr, 1000 credits), Lifetime ($199 one-time, 1000 credits)
- Credit packages: Basic (100), Standard (200), Premium (500)
- Webhook handler validates signature and distributes credits on payment completion
- Checkout flow: server action → create Stripe customer if needed → record payment → redirect to Stripe → webhook updates record

### Credits System
- Core logic in `apps/web/src/credits/`
- 7 transaction types: `MONTHLY_REFRESH`, `REGISTER_GIFT`, `PURCHASE_PACKAGE`, `SUBSCRIPTION_RENEWAL`, `LIFETIME_MONTHLY`, `USAGE`, `EXPIRE`
- Credits tracked in `userCredit` table with history in `creditTransaction`
- Monthly distribution via `bun run distribute-credits` script

### Provider Pattern (used throughout)
All external integrations follow a pluggable provider pattern with factory functions:
- Payment: `apps/web/src/payment/` (Stripe)
- Mail: `apps/web/src/mail/` (Resend, React Email templates — all localized)
- Notifications: `apps/web/src/notification/` (Discord, Feishu)
- Storage: `apps/web/src/storage/` (S3 via `s3mini`)
- AI: `apps/web/src/ai/` (multiple image generation providers)

### State & Data Flow
- Server components fetch data directly; mutations via server actions
- Zustand stores in `apps/web/src/stores/` for client-side state
- React Query for async data on client
- Forms use React Hook Form + Zod validation

### Configuration
- Centralized app config with feature flags: `apps/web/src/config/website.tsx`
- Demo mode: `NEXT_PUBLIC_DEMO_WEBSITE` env var (enables Crisp chat, Turnstile CAPTCHA, looser admin checks)
- Environment template: `apps/web/.dev.vars.example`; copy it to ignored `apps/web/.dev.vars` for local OpenNext/Cloudflare development

## Code Style

- Biome enforces: 2-space indentation, 80-char line width, single quotes, ES5 trailing commas, semicolons required
- Filenames: kebab-case (`dashboard-sidebar.tsx`); hooks prefixed `use-` (`use-session.ts`)
- Named exports preferred; default exports only for pages/layouts
- Server-only code marked with `"use server"` directive
- Tailwind CSS v4 with tokens in `apps/web/src/styles/`
- UI primitives from Radix UI; icons from `lucide-react`
- Conventional Commits: `feat:`, `fix:`, `chore:`

## File Editing Rules (Strict)

These rules apply to every code change, not just the first iteration.

- Never overwrite or rewrite source files via shell heredocs, scripted writes,
  or one-shot file dumps. The following patterns are forbidden:
  - `python3 - <<'PY' ... p.write_text(...)` or any equivalent Python/Node
    heredoc that pipes a full file body into `Path(...).write_text`,
    `Bun.write`, `fs.writeFile`, etc.
  - `cat > path/to/file <<'EOF' ... EOF`, `echo "..." > file`,
    `sed -i 's/.../.../' file` for content edits.
  - Inline `bun -e "await Bun.write(...)"` or similar one-liners that produce
    or replace TypeScript/TSX/JSON sources.
- Use the `StrReplace` tool for targeted edits, the `Write` tool only when
  creating a brand-new file from scratch, and the `Delete` tool to remove a
  file. Read the file first when context is needed; do not guess.
- For batch edits across files, run `StrReplace` per file with `replace_all`
  when appropriate, instead of generating files via shell scripts.
- Only `bun` (or its CLI equivalents like `bun run`, `bunx --bun`) may run
  package or build commands. Do not introduce ad-hoc shell text-mangling
  utilities to mutate project sources.

## UI Component Rules

- Always prefer shadcn/ui primitives in `apps/web/src/components/ui` over hand-rolled
  markup. When a component is missing, install via
  `bunx --bun shadcn@latest add <name>` from `apps/web` instead of building
  a custom div.
- Do not commit non-shadcn registry mirrors (e.g. raw `magicui`, `tailark`,
  `animate-ui`, `diceui` source dumps). If a registry component is needed,
  install it on-demand and edit the resulting `apps/web/src/components/ui/*` file.
- Reuse the project layout primitives: `Container`, `SiteHeader`, `SiteFooter`
  for marketing/public pages; `DashboardSidebar` for protected pages.

payload.aggregated_output:
# Skills ai-package.json Decisions

This document records the current v1 decisions for using `ai-package.json` to
manage skills. Plugins are intentionally out of scope for this round and will
be designed later.

## Summary

- `ai-package.json` is the user-editable manifest for skills.
- `ai-package.json` is the primary interface; `skills-lock.json` is only for
  compatibility or installed-state tracking.
- The top-level key is `skills`, not the singular `skill` used by the current
  tiktok schema.
- Each skill entry is keyed by the installed skill name.
- The skill name can differ from the source directory name.

## Manifest Shape

```json
{
  "skills": {
    "find-skills": {
      "source": "github:vercel-labs/skills",
      "version": "main@df0579f85cb8a360473c921e1343359006100d3c",
      "path": "skills/find-skills"
    },
    "grill-me": {
      "source": "file:.",
      "path": ".agents/skills/grille-me"
    }
  }
}
```

## Field Rules

### `source`

`source` is a locator string in the form `<provider>:<package-id>`.

Supported v1 providers:

- `github:owner/repo`
- `gitlab:group/repo`
- `marketplace:owner/package`
- `file:relative-or-absolute-path`

### `version`

`version` is required for remote sources.

For Git-backed sources, use `<ref>@<commitSha>`.

Example:

```json
"version": "main@df0579f85cb8a360473c921e1343359006100d3c"
```

Installation must use the commit sha as the source of truth. The ref exists to
make future updates understandable and to provide the update source.

`file:` sources may omit `version`.

### `path`

`path` is required. It points to the skill directory inside the source package
or repository.

The target directory must contain `SKILL.md`.

The installer should not auto-discover `SKILL.md`; the manifest must make the
skill location explicit.

## Validation Examples

Valid:

- `github:vercel-labs/skills` with `main@<sha>` and `skills/find-skills`
- `file:.` with `.agents/skills/grille-me`

Invalid:

- A remote source without `version`
- An entry without `path`
- A `path` that does not contain `SKILL.md`

## Notes

- `skills-lock.json` should not become the main user-authored configuration.
- The older tiktok `ai-package.md` design is too stateful for this manifest
  because it tracks installed files and timestamps.
- Plugins are deferred so that the skills interface can stay small and stable.

- Plugins are deferred so that the skills interface can stay small and stable.

payload.message:
已完成 CLI 这轮实现。

主要变更：
- 用 `cac` 重做入口：[apps/cli/src/cli.ts](/Users/bytedance/dev/ai-skills/apps/cli/src/cli.ts)
- 平铺拆分模块：`manifest` / `install` / `git` / `install-command` / `types`
- 用 `ts-pattern` 处理 provider/progress/summary 分支
- 支持从 `ai-package.json` 安装 `github:`、`gitlab:`、`file:` skills 到 `.agents/skills`
- Git 远端安装按 `version: <ref>@<commitSha>` 里的 commit sha checkout
- 加了 Vitest 测试和 tmp dir 端到端测试：[apps/cli/src/tests](/Users/bytedance/dev/ai-skills/apps/cli/src/tests)
- CLI coverage 输出已加入 ignore：`.gitignore`

验证结果：
- `bun run type-check:cli` 通过
- `bun run test` 通过：6 files / 36 tests，单文件覆盖率阈值 95%+ 通过
- `bunx biome check apps/cli/index.ts apps/cli/src apps/cli/package.json apps/cli/vitest.config.ts .gitignore` 通过
- `bun run lint` 仍失败，但原因是现有 ignored `submodule/tiktok-skills/.../biome.json` 嵌套 root 配置冲突，不是本次 CLI 文件问题

注意：`marketplace:` 目前是显式拒绝安装，因为文档没有定义 marketplace resolver/下载端点，我没有伪装成 GitHub fallback。

目标已标记完成。用时：343 秒。
